top_signals.title: ECB Risks Repeating 2011 Mistake With Rate Hike, Economists Warn
top_signals.summary: The European Central Bank’s resolve to uphold its inflation-quashing reputation risks luring it into a damaging error when it meets this week, economists warn.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: FirstFT: AI-powered espionage triggers Putin camera scare
top_signals.summary: Also in today’s newsletter: Iran deal and UK bank taxes
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Goldman Sees Rupee Weakness Capped After Steps to Boost Inflows
top_signals.summary: The Indian rupee’s slide may be nearing a floor after authorities unveiled measures to boost foreign inflows, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Voters sour on Trump’s handling of inflation and grocery prices — FT poll - Financial Times
top_signals.summary: Voters sour on Trump’s handling of inflation and grocery prices — FT poll  Financial Times
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Xi arrives in Pyongyang for summit with North Korea’s Kim
top_signals.summary: In a veiled swipe at Japan ahead of the talks, Chinese leader Xi Jinping urged Pyongyang to join Beijing in rejecting "any scheme or action aimed at reviving militarism."
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Work to improve safety continues 25 years after mass stabbing at Osaka school
top_signals.summary: On June 8, 2001, a man armed with knives broke into the school and stabbed eight children to death, and injured scores of others.
